required_report: At least one random budget below and above the method's realized human cost; report realized best-step human cost, not only nominal budget.
why_it_matters: Prevents a learned trigger from appearing efficient only because the random baseline was mismatched.
current_evidence: R021 random_b350 dominates LV-VoI scale3; random_b450/b600 define nearby frontier

required_report: Evaluate selected checkpoints with repeated episodes; report binomial confidence intervals and seed variability.
why_it_matters: Single restored or final evaluations can change rankings in noisy RL settings.
current_evidence: R020-R024 use 5 x 20 repeated checkpoint evaluation

required_report: Report raw final policy, restored best policy, and best-step human cost.
why_it_matters: Separates late collapse/checkpoint selection from true intervention efficiency.
current_evidence: R020-R024 summaries include best-step cost and final/restored policy fields

required_report: Log intervention starts with timing, budget fraction, task geometry, score, and p_fail when available.
why_it_matters: Explains how a trigger spends budget and tests mechanism hypotheses.
current_evidence: R023/R024 traces reveal over-triggering and score/timing mismatch

required_report: Before expanding a repaired trigger, compare it against same-seed cost-matched random using repeated evaluation.
why_it_matters: Stops weak heuristic repairs before expensive expansion and prevents over-tuning.
current_evidence: R022/R024 stopped after dominance by same-seed random_b350
